steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence contains an opening em dash after "that": "conservation efforts that—through community involvement and scientific ______". A paired em dash interruption must be closed by a second em dash before the main verb "aim" resumes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'study—': closes the opening em dash with a matching em dash, correctly bracketing the parenthetical phrase and allowing "aim to bring adjutants back" to continue the sentence. ✓
  • Choice B — 'study,': a comma cannot close an em dash interruption — the punctuation types must match. ✗
  • Choice C — 'study:': a colon cannot close an em dash interruption, and a colon here would create a grammatical break where none is intended. ✗
  • Choice D — 'study': no closing punctuation leaves the em dash interruption unclosed, making the sentence ungrammatical. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
